generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T AGARICUS SEMIGLOBATUS. A. semiglobatus, Batsch. Common on dung, England and United States. Stem, 2-3 inches high. Authority. Poisoning cases. ► Spirits exhilarated. ► Stupor. ► Tongue parched; slightly streaked with white. ► Severe pains in bowels, increased by pressure, with violent purging and vomiting. ► Urine scanty. ► Miscarriage in second month. ► Pulse 100-120. ► Fatal convulsions.
